title: Phymosia pauciflora
what_description: Phymosia pauciflora is a species of flowering plant in the family Malvaceae. It is a shrub or small tree that grows up to 3 meters in height, with a woody stem and a smooth, grayish-brown bark. The leaves are alternate, elliptical, and 5-8 cm long, with a pointed tip and a serrated margin. The flowers are pink or purple, 5-petaled, and 2-3 cm in diameter, arranged in axillary clusters. The plant is endemic to Mexico and Central America, where it grows in tropical and subtropical dry forests.
